scenes.voiceover: You see something and buy it immediately.
No thinking. No waiting.
Shopping feels good in the moment.
That quick dopamine hit.
But later, you regret it.
The bills pile up.
Impulse spending fills an emotional need, not a real one.
You're buying the feeling, not the thing.
American consumer culture is designed to trigger this.
One-click ordering. Buy now, pay later. Flash sales.
The whole system bypasses your rational brain and goes straight to emotions.
The average American has thousands in credit card debt from small impulse purchases that added up.
That $5 coffee, $15 lunch, $30 shirt.
Over a year, thousands.
Impulse spending is also a coping mechanism.
Bored? Buy something.
Stressed? Buy something.
Your brain craves that dopamine hit.
Breaking this pattern means inserting a pause.
Wait 24 hours before buying anything non-essential.
Most of the time, the urge passes and you save money.
